title: Code Approaches Example
subtitle: Compare implementation approaches and recommend one path.

sections.heading: Code Approaches in use
sections.body: Use this output when a user needs to compare credible implementation paths before code is written. The example weighs a static-export renderer, a route-backed preview, and a hybrid payload pipeline.

cards.title: Static renderer
cards.body: Best when the artifact must be portable, reviewable, and safe to attach to a PR.

cards.title: Route-backed preview
cards.body: Best when the artifact depends on app state, auth, or live product components.

cards.title: Hybrid payload
cards.body: Best when source material should be structured once, then rendered into several formats.

rows: The artifact must travel through email, Slack, or PR comments
Needs a dev server or deployment
The demo is part of the product surface
The same material needs gallery, print, and handoff outputs

steps.title: Normalize source material
steps.body: Extract title, audience, sections, diagrams, sources, and print requirements before choosing layout details.

steps.title: Render as portable HTML
steps.body: Use the VPK HTML shell when the artifact needs to be opened offline or attached outside the app.

steps.title: Promote only proven patterns
steps.body: Move a pattern into the main app only after a demo shows that the interaction and reading rhythm work.

sections.heading: Example data policy
sections.body: This demo uses invented project data so the template shape is visible without depending on private tickets, customer names, or live metrics.
